items.definition_en: An act is a part of a play or show.
items.example_en: The play has three acts.

items.definition_en: An audience is a group of people watching a show.
items.example_en: The audience cheered loudly after the performance.

items.definition_en: An actor is a person who performs in plays or movies.
items.example_en: The actor played the main character.

items.definition_en: An actress is a woman who acts in plays or movies.
items.example_en: The actress accepted her prize on stage.

items.definition_en: Ballet is a style of dance performed on stage.
items.example_en: We watched a ballet at the theater last night.

items.definition_en: A curtain is a large piece of cloth that opens or closes the stage.
items.example_en: The curtain closed after the final scene.

items.definition_en: A drama is a serious play for the theater.
items.example_en: They performed a drama about family life.

items.definition_en: A playwright is a person who writes plays.
items.example_en: The famous playwright visited the theater.

items.definition_en: A prop is an object used by actors during a play.
items.example_en: The chair was used as a prop in the scene.

items.definition_en: A script is the written text of a play or movie.
items.example_en: She read her lines from the script.

items.definition_en: A stage is the area where actors perform.
items.example_en: The stage was decorated with flowers.

items.definition_en: A theater is a place where plays and shows happen.
items.example_en: We went to the theater to see a play.

items.definition_en: A rehearsal is a practice before the real show.
items.example_en: The cast had a rehearsal every day.

items.definition_en: A cast is all the actors in a play or movie.
items.example_en: The cast took a bow at the end.

items.definition_en: An audition is a test where someone tries to get a part in a show.
items.example_en: He went to an audition for a new play.

items.definition_en: A ticket is a small paper or card letting you enter a show.
items.example_en: I bought my ticket at the door.

items.definition_en: A play is a story performed on stage by actors.
items.example_en: They saw a play about history.

items.definition_en: A puppet is a small figure moved by a person in a show.
items.example_en: Children laughed at the puppet show.

items.definition_en: A mask is something worn on the face by actors.
items.example_en: The actors wore masks during the performance.

items.definition_en: A box office is where you buy tickets at the theater.
items.example_en: She worked at the box office selling tickets.
